1.Text: {#Emph}There is an old adage which goes, one would be wise never to strike a sleeping Cyclops, and instead carefully walk by; but at times, exceptions must be made.
I might make one of those exceptions now...
{#Emph}If once this was a pasture in which livestock grazed and roamed, now little evidence of this remains; only the mud, and half-burnt vestiges of a more-peaceful time.
Polyphemus should be tending to this place...
{#Emph}The noxious, death-strewn field beyond the city of Ephyra now lies still once more, although a sense of rising Fear yet lingers with the many restless souls that there reside.
Lady Medea certainly transformed this place.
{#Emph}On such particularly fearsome nights, the Witch of Shadows is believed to haunt this awfully putrescent field, whilst conducting her profane experiments upon the dead.
Still feel a little nauseous after that...
How... can... I...?
You.
Through Ephyra as before...
Best have a look around...
Something's changed...

2.Text: They're trying to slow me down...

16.Text: Just a few Pylons to go.

1.Text: Now to get out of here.

6.Text: Should be able to get out.

1.Text: {#Emph}The pathway to the docks is sealed shut by a phantasmal force that neither mortal nor immortal strength can budge.
It's drawing strength from Shades...
{#Emph}A barricade bars passage to the docks beyond the city square, leaving the denizens of the city of Ephyra confined to their ruinous environs.
We'll get through. Even if it takes every Shade in this city.
{#Emph}The barely-held-together city is a labyrinth of streets, some barricaded still, yet others not, each horror-filled, night after endless night.
So many mortals lived and died in such a place...
{#Emph}A mortal-made stone likeness of the grim lord of the dead stands in the center of the city, looking on at the devastation sown as if in stunned silence.
This is my... Father...
Vengeance for my father.
{#Emph}Amid the stench of death lies a rejuvenating spring, etched from a block of stone into the likeness of the verdure-goddess and the Underworld's rightful queen.
This fountain... Mother...
If it isn't the boar of my dreams...!

4.Text: You don't remember me, do you?

6.Text: You don't like staying down for long.

1.Text: {#Emph}Too many hunters failed to tame the massive raging Boar of Erymanthus whilst it lived and breathed; and not even Death has been able to still its mighty hooves and tusks.
I did, though! For now.
My curse... I feel... all right...
{#Emph}The light of the Moon is all that now illuminates the surface, where the dead have risen, and Olympus teeters on the brink of doom.
Damn you, Chronos...
{#Emph}Few entrances into the Underworld exist, and one of them is through the Acheron, whose accursed waters bear the weight of all the woes of mortalkind.
If our foes can use this as an exit, so can I.
{#Emph}As the ill-fated princess feels her life-force fade with every breath of surface air, her sole recourse is to return whence she came.
...What, I... {#Emph}ungh...
Must be where Polyphemus lives... {#Emph}eugh...

4.Text: How does he live like this?

1.Text: {#Emph}Across a foul-smelling hovel crudely furnished with assorted fixtures of exaggerated proportion, and strewn with remains both fresh and long-decayed, comes a waft of sea-borne air...
I smell mostly the decay...
{#Emph}Unfortunate livestock residing with the ravenous Cyclops seem to be unaware of the conditions under which they graze about and wander, not exactly lost; at least, not yet.
The one they have to guide them is asleep...
{#Emph}Doomed city at her back, the Princess of the Dead heads toward a dismal pasture, where she senses she is not exactly being watched.
Polyphemus must already know I'm here...
{#Emph}A lonesome, dusty path from the necropolis exhibits much of the decay within, yet provides comfort of a sort to all the many simple Shades nearby with nowhere left to go.
We'll find you a new home.
{#Emph}Across the River Acheron, the Underworld Boatman travels between realms, through hidden borders, unseen as a shadow in the dark.
Is there anywhere Lord Charon cannot row?
{#Emph}Nrrgghhh...
The dead don't seem to want to leave...
Gap in the wall...
{#Emph}The city of Ephyra, which once stood in solemn tribute to the fear-inducing Underworld King and Queen, now barely stands at all.
...Yet it stands in my way.
{#Emph}In one forgotten recess of the fallen city, a solitary seeker of profane mysteries conducts her undertakings beneath a pale, distant Moon.
I've learned a curse or two from this one...
A pox upon our enemies.
